body:
## Review Summary

The load test refactoring (burst/TPS split, genesis generator) is well-structured and the new `tps_load_test` design with round-robin accounts and local nonce tracking is solid. However, there are two compile-blocking issues that need fixing before merge: `HashMap::new()` where `BTreeMap` is required (line 625) and `clap::value_parser!(Network)` without a `FromStr` impl (line 92). See inline comments for details.

This branch is ~4 months old â€” please rebase on `main` and verify it compiles before requesting re-review.

comments.body:
`GenesisAccount.storage` is `BTreeMap<U256, U256>`, but this passes `HashMap::new()`. This won't compile.

```suggestion
                    storage: Default::default(),
```
Or explicitly `BTreeMap::new()` with the appropriate import.

`clap::value_parser!(Network)` requires `Network` to implement `FromStr` (or `ValueEnum`), but `Network` only has `From<&str>`. This won't compile as-is.

Either add `impl FromStr for Network` in `ethrex-config`, or use a custom value parser here, e.g.:
```rust
value_parser = |s: &str| -> Result<Network, String> { Ok(Network::from(s)) },
```

`tps_load_test` runs an infinite `loop` and never returns `Ok(())` â€” it can only exit via `?` propagation or a panic. This means the caller's elapsed-time printout (line ~594) is dead code when `LoadType::Tps` is selected. Consider either:
- Adding a `--duration` flag so the loop exits after a set time, or
- Printing periodic stats inside the loop (e.g., every 10s), since the final summary will never be reached.

nit: `i64::MAX as u64` is an unusual way to express "maximum willingness to pay". Consider `u64::MAX` directly, or better yet, fetch the current base fee and apply a multiplier â€” this avoids potentially overpaying if the load test is ever pointed at a real network.

The `"local-devnet-l2"` arm is added before the `GenesisPath` fallback, which is correct. However, note the comment above says "we don't allow to manually specify the local devnet genesis" â€” this new arm intentionally allows it for the L2 variant. Might be worth updating that comment to clarify the distinction.
